description: A designed 'Wall of Honor' memorial tribute graphic in landscape format honoring a fallen officer. The background is a dark, moody, desaturated stone/slate texture; along the right edge is a soft-focus photograph of a furled American flag (red, white, blue stars visible) with a bouquet of deep-red roses in the lower-right corner, evoking a funeral/remembrance mood. At top-left, large elegant white serif typography reads 'Wall of Honor' (with 'of' in italic script). Beside the headline is the ICE memorial ribbon emblem (a blue-and-white ribbon with the round ICE/DHS seal at its center). Centered in the image is a rectangular black inset photo of a gold law-enforcement badge: a classic eagle-topped shield reading 'U.S. CUSTOMS / THE DEPARTMENT OF THE / TREASURY' across the top banner, a central Treasury seal with '1789', and 'SPECIAL AGENT' on the bottom banner. Lower-left text in white serif reads 'Special Agent / Timothy M. O'Riordan', a thin gold divider, then 'End of Watch' (with 'of' italic), and the date 'Tuesday, September 29, 1964'. The composition is solemn, formal and reverential. Messaging technique: institutional legacy-building and valorization of agents who died in the line of duty, tying modern ICE to the historical U.S. Customs/Treasury enforcement lineage. Target audience: ICE personnel, law-enforcement community, and the public, to evoke respect, sacrifice, and continuity of mission.
notable_text: Wall of Honor; Special Agent Timothy M. O'Riordan; End of Watch; Tuesday, September 29, 1964; U.S. CUSTOMS - THE DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY - SPECIAL AGENT - 1789
